source: Analysis of 5 ETS reference sets (20 questions) from goarno.io
description: Flavor model for Take an Interview question generation. Defines the statistical profile of authentic ETS-style interview sets.

structure.intro_template: You have agreed to participate in a [study/survey/research project] about [topic].

Q1.notes: Includes 'Thank you for participating' opener + personal experience question

Q2.notes: Comparative or challenge-focused, asks for examples

Q3.notes: Presents a debatable claim + asks for opinion, often 'Some people/experts argue that...'

Q4.notes: Future-oriented or policy recommendation, longest question

Q1_personal_factual.asks_about: current habits, recent experiences, personal situation
Q1_personal_factual.complexity: low — answerable from personal life, no debate required

Q2_descriptive_comparative.asks_about: advantages vs disadvantages, challenges, differences between approaches
question_forms: How does X compare to Y...
What are some advantages and disadvantages...
What challenges do people face when...
Q2_descriptive_comparative.complexity: medium — requires structured comparison, examples expected

Q3_analytical_opinion.asks_about: agree or disagree with claim, perspective, extent of agreement
question_forms: To what extent do you agree...
Do you think this approach is effective...
Q3_analytical_opinion.complexity: high — must take a position and defend it with reasoning

Q4_evaluative_predictive.asks_about: future trends, policy suggestions, how things will change
question_forms: How do you think X will change in the next...
What policies or programs would you suggest...
Q4_evaluative_predictive.complexity: highest — requires forward thinking, recommendation, or prediction

clear_topic_framing.description: Each set has an explicit topic and scenario intro
indicators: all 4 questions relate to same topic

difficulty_progression.description: Smooth escalation from personal to evaluative

open_ended.description: All questions invite extended 45-second responses, never yes/no
indicators: no questions answerable in one word

debatable_Q3.description: Q3 presents a claim that reasonable people could disagree on

future_oriented_Q4.description: Q4 asks about what will or should happen next

anti_patterns.yes_no_questions: Never ask a question answerable with just yes or no
anti_patterns.leading_questions: Never embed the expected answer in the question framing
anti_patterns.culturally_biased: Avoid topics requiring specific cultural knowledge (holidays, specific sports, religion)
anti_patterns.too_academic: Questions should be about everyday experiences, not specialized knowledge
anti_patterns.topic_drift: All 4 questions must stay on the same topic — no mid-set topic changes
anti_patterns.identical_openers: Avoid starting Q2/Q3/Q4 with the same word
